title: End-to-end repair for context slimming real sampling and remote result persistence
objective: Close the entire blocked chain around real LLM sampling for context slimming. Do not stop at pre-checks. Run the full repair flow needed to reach one definitive outcome: either recover a real remote terminal result for the sampling task, or prove with evidence that the remote executor currently has a result-persistence problem that blocks completion.

references.description: Task that bootstrapped controller env and retried the real-sampling dispatch.
Previous recovery task that stopped too early after only the pre-reset check.
Latest incomplete recovery result. It says no reset/redispatch were used, but the evidence folder also contains reset and redispatch responses.

remote_task.expected_goal: Run one real worker sample and one real manager sample with slimmed prompt path; capture real prompt/token evidence; verify trusted broadcast/worker evidence/manager gate do not regress.

required_work.description: Read fresh /api/v1/state and /api/v1/results. If the target task already has a terminal result, stop recovery and collect it immediately.
Compare structured-result claims with evidence files. If prior evidence already shows reset and redispatch happened, do not keep reporting 'pre-reset only'. Use evidence as the source of truth.
If the target task is still running and /api/v1/results still lacks a matching terminal result, classify this specifically as a remote result-chain / result-persistence repair case.

If the task remains running without a result after a reasonable poll window, perform one controlled POST /api/v1/admin/reset-running-task with an explicit reason describing stale running task plus missing terminal result.

Before re-dispatching the expensive real-sampling task again, dispatch one tiny executor smoke task whose only job is to return a strict JSON terminal result. Verify whether that result appears in /api/v1/results.

If the smoke task itself cannot produce a terminal result, stop and classify the blocker as a remote executor result-persistence problem. If the smoke task produces a terminal result successfully, continue.
After a passing smoke test, re-dispatch the real-sampling task exactly once and poll long enough to either recover its terminal result or reach a precise blocker.
If the real-sampling terminal result is recovered, collect worker/manager sample facts, prompt/token evidence, and regression outcomes. If not recovered, classify the first blocker precisely.
Write detailed logs and publish one final structured result that reflects what actually happened, not an early partial snapshot.

do_not: Do not stop after only reading pre-reset state/results again.
Do not report 'UsedReset=false' or 'UsedRedispatch=false' if the evidence files already show reset/redispatch happened.
Do not spam multiple resets or multiple redispatches. Keep recovery controlled and bounded.
Do not redispatch the expensive real-sampling task again before proving that the executor can currently return terminal results through /api/v1/results.
Do not expand this task into UI work, new features, or unrelated protocol changes.
acceptance: Either the real-sampling task yields a recovered terminal result with evidence, or the remote executor result-persistence problem is proven with evidence from a smoke task.
If reset is used, the reset response is captured.
If smoke task is used, its dispatch/state/results chain is captured.
If redispatch is used, the redispatch response and terminal outcome are captured.
The final structured result is consistent with the evidence files.

deliverables.description: Evidence folder for the full repair chain.
Main-thread log root for detailed logs.

working_principles: Evidence beats narration. If file evidence and a summary conflict, trust the evidence and say so.
A task that is accepted and running but never appears in /api/v1/results is a server repair problem until proven otherwise.
Prove executor result persistence with a cheap smoke task before burning more retries on the expensive real-sampling task.
The goal of this task is closure, not another shallow status note.
